growth_records.medium: MODG (MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose)
growth_records.cite: Duport et al. TSPO safeguards porphyrin–iron balance under anoxic conditions in Bacillus cereus. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01738-25. [PMC13185565]
growth_records.snippet: µmax (h−1) 0.96 ± 0.05*; Y biomass/glucose (g·g−1) 2.01 ± 0.27; Yields (mol/mol glucose): Lactate 1.34±0.12, Acetate 0.19±0.02*, Formate 0.42±0.02, Ethanol 0.15±0.06 (Table 1, WT)
growth_records.notes: µmax=0.96 h-1 reported directly in Table 1 (anaerobic batch, MODG, pH 7.2, 37°C, modified Gompertz fit). Doubling time = ln2/0.96 = 0.72 h (calculated). Glucose uptake qS = µmax/Yxs = 0.96 h-1 / 2.01 g biomass/g glucose = 0.4776 g glucose/gDW/h -> /180.16 g/mol *1000 = 2.65 mmol/gDW/h (calculated). Product secretion qP = qS(glucose,mmol/gDW/h) x molar yield (mol product/mol glucose, from Table 1): lactate 2.65x1.34=3.55, acetate 2.65x0.19=0.50, formate 2.65x0.42=1.11, ethanol 2.65x0.15=0.40 mmol/gDW/h (all calculated, 2-step derivation). Note: the paper's stated Y biomass/glucose (2.01 g/g) is numerically higher than typical biomass yields (usually <1 g/g); value used as given without correction, so downstream derived rates carry that uncertainty.

growth_records.snippet: µmax (h−1) 0.64 ± 0.14*; Y biomass/glucose (g·g−1) 1.86 ± 0.23; Yields (mol/mol glucose): Lactate 1.44±0.15, Acetate 0.28±0.05*, Formate 0.41±0.09, Ethanol 0.12±0.05 (Table 1, ΔtspO)
growth_records.notes: µmax=0.64 h-1 reported directly in Table 1 for ΔtspO mutant (anaerobic batch, MODG, pH 7.2, 37°C). Doubling time = ln2/0.64 = 1.08 h (calculated). Glucose uptake qS = 0.64/1.86 = 0.3441 g glucose/gDW/h -> 1.91 mmol/gDW/h (calculated). Secretion qP = qS x molar yield from Table 1: lactate 1.91x1.44=2.75, acetate 1.91x0.28=0.53, formate 1.91x0.41=0.78, ethanol 1.91x0.12=0.23 mmol/gDW/h (calculated, 2-step derivation same caveats as WT record re: Y value magnitude).

sec.method: specific productivity q_PHB, gravimetric P3HB / dry biomass (Table 1A)

growth_records.cite: Morales-Núñez, Isabo et al. Comparison of poly-3-hydroxybutyrate (P3HB) synthesis by Bacillus cereus and Azotobacter vinelandii OP: effect of agitation on the accumulation and physicochemical properties of the biopolymer. Bioresources and Bioprocessing (2025). DOI:10.1186/s40643-025-00978-2. [PMC12718266]
growth_records.notes: P3HB is an intracellular storage polyester (product), reported as volumetric Q_PHB (mg L-1 h-1) and specific q_PHB (mg gDW-1 h-1) in Table 1A; both experimental. Biomass (gravimetric dry weight) 0.619 g/L; P3HB 0.02 g/L (text). No sucrose/O2 uptake rate and no growth rate reported; mu not derivable (only endpoint gravimetric biomass, x0 unknown). Initial pH 6.87 rose to 9.09 by end (all conditions) due to amino-acid deamination in LB. Temp 30 C (inoculation temp; cultures on same orbital shaker). kLa 41.73 h-1 is theoretical (correlation), not measured.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MSM) with benzophenone as sole carbon source

up.method: GC-MS (Agilent 7890B, LOD 0.1 mg/L); from 20 mg/L initial
pseudo-second-order (Lagergren) kinetic fit; DK2 best fit PSO2

sec.compound: BZP degradation metabolites (phenyl cyclohexyl ketone, benzophenone dimethyl ketal, 2-cyclohexen-1-one, benzcarbothioic acid, heptacosane, hydroxylation/demethylation products)
sec.method: GC-MS/NIST library identification (match >900); qualitative, no rates

growth_records.cite: Maqsood A et al. Isolation, identification and subsequent application of Gram-positive bacterial strains for the bioremediation of benzophenone. RSC Advances (2025). DOI:10.1039/d5ra05131b. [PMC12642988]
growth_records.snippet: It was found that DK2 and S4 had similar doubling time, of 1.7 hours... DK2 showed 67.5% degradation... they showed bacterial growth of 1.0 x 10^5 +/- 10^3 ... CFU mL-1 after 72 h.
growth_records.notes: Bacillus cereus DK2, aerobic batch, 30C, pH 7, 150 rpm, MSM + 20 mg/L BZP as sole carbon source. Doubling time reported = 1.7 h; growth_rate mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/1.7 = 0.408 h-1 (CALCULATED). Lag phase 4 h. Biomass 1.0e5 CFU/mL at 72 h. BZP (substrate) degradation 67.5% in 72 h by GC-MS (from 20 mg/L). Could not derive qS in mmol/gCDW/h: biomass reported as CFU/mL not gDW, and BZP consumed = 0.675*20 mg/L / (182.22 g/mol) over 72 h = ~0.074 mM but no gDW biomass to normalize. Kinetics: DK2 best fit pseudo-second-order, rate constant K2=0.9 mg h-1.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MSM) with benzophenone; optimized conditions with yeast extract

up.method: GC-MS; consortium in liquid MSM under aerobic conditions
GC-MS; under optimum conditions (pH 7, 30C, 1.0 g/L NH4NO3, 2.0 g/L yeast extract)

growth_records.cite: Maqsood A et al. Isolation, identification and subsequent application of Gram-positive bacterial strains for the bioremediation of benzophenone. RSC Advances (2025). DOI:10.1039/d5ra05131b. [PMC12642988]
growth_records.snippet: The consortium (DK2 and S4) showed 86% degradation after 72 hours of incubation in liquid MSM under aerobic conditions. The BZP degradation reached 100% +/- 2.35% after 96 h under the optimum environmental conditions.
growth_records.notes: Co-culture (DK2 + B. pumilus S4) consortium condition. BZP degradation 86% in 72 h (MSM, sole carbon) and 100%+/-2.35% in 96 h under optimum (pH 7, 30C, 1.0 g/L ammonium nitrate as N source, 2.0 g/L yeast extract as additional carbon). Aerobic batch, 150 rpm. No separate growth-rate value for the consortium in text. Substrate consumption expressed as % degradation (GC-MS), not mmol/gCDW/h.

up.method: LC-MS exometabolomic max depletion rate normalized to gCDW

growth_records.cite: Erbilgin, Onur et al. Dynamic substrate preferences predict metabolic properties of a simple microbial consortium. BMC Bioinformatics (2017). DOI:10.1186/s12859-017-1478-2. [PMC5259839]
growth_records.method: OD600 monitored (gCDW via k=0.9 gCDW/L/OD); numeric µmax not given in main text (per-compound sole-source µ in Table S1, not in XML)
growth_records.snippet: We observed these rates to be less than 400 mMol/hour/[gCDW] except for glucose depletion by Bc, which we calculated to be about 5079 mMol/hour/gCDW ... various studies ... measured the glucose uptake rate to range from 2 to 60 mMol/hour/gC
growth_records.notes: Bacillus cereus (Bc) monoculture, batch, defined glucose+19-aa medium, aerobic, 30C, 200 rpm. Max glucose depletion rate ~5079 mmol/gCDW/h (LC-MS, measured) — authors flag this as anomalously high vs literature 2-60 mmol/gCDW/h and as likely a targeted-analysis artifact (glucose depleted ~2.4 h before biomass increase; possibly extracellular transformation to glycogen). Other 19 substrates depleted at <400 mmol/gCDW/h (per-compound values in Additional file 3: Table S1, not in XML). Grew on glucose as sole C source despite genome-predicted auxotrophy for Lys/Phe/Tyr/His/Ser. No µmax numeric in main text.

growth_records.cite: Senouci-Rezkallah, Khadidja et al. Adaptive responses of Bacillus cereus ATCC14579 cells upon exposure to acid conditions involve ATPase activity to maintain their internal pH. MicrobiologyOpen (2015). DOI:10.1002/mbo3.239. [PMC4398511]
growth_records.snippet: Bacillus cereus cells were grown in anaerobic chemostat culture at external pH (pHe) 7.0 or 5.5 and at a growth rate of 0.2 h-1. ... All experiments were carried out at 34C with agitation at 300 rpm. Culture pH was monitored and maintained
growth_records.notes: Acid-adapted condition. Anaerobic chemostat at controlled pHe 5.5, 34C, D=growth rate mu=0.2 h-1 (author-set steady-state; mu=D at chemostat steady state, hence growth_method=reported). Doubling time calculated: td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.2=3.466 h -> 3.47 h. No substrate uptake, product secretion, or MFA fluxes are reported in this paper; it measures F1F0-ATPase activity (e.g. 9.38 +/- 0.3 U/mg protein at pHe 5.5, 5.24 +/- 0.1 U/mg at pHe 7.0, where U = umol/L Pi per min per mg protein) rather than exchange rates, so uptake/secretion/mfa arrays are empty. J-Broth (JB) medium composition is cited from Claus and Berkeley 1986, not given in-text, so medium_composition_stated=false.
